title: Projectiles, Edge Triggers, Cooldowns, Lifetimes, and Reuse · Behavior Case: Fire Intent
summary: Held fire spawns unbounded bullets, cooldown uses frame count, expired bullets remain in collision groups, pool reuse keeps old velocity, and rapid restart leaks projectiles. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fire intent increment in bounded projectile lifecycle. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.
Make ship, hazard, projectile, focus, damage, and status distinguishable without color alone and under reduced effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-fire-intent — Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-projectiles-cooldowns-pools-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_projectiles_cooldowns_pools_5(requested_shots: int = 12, accepted_shots: int = 5, cooldown_ms: int = 180, lifetime_ms: int = 900, live_cap: int = 6, leaked_projectiles: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if requested_shots < 0 or accepted_shots < 0 or cooldown_ms < 1:
        return False, accepted_shots, "projectile-domain"
    if accepted_shots > requested_shots or accepted_shots > live_cap:
        return False, accepted_shots, "fire-budget"
    if lifetime_ms <= cooldown_ms:
        return False, lifetime_ms, "lifetime-policy"
    if leaked_projectiles > 0:
        return False, leaked_projectiles, "ownership-leak"
    assert accepted_shots <= live_cap
    return True, accepted_shots, "bounded-projectiles"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fire Intent model · Theory Fire Intent
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Fire Intent: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9090 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate edge-triggered firing on cooldown, lifetime, live-count, reuse, and deterministic projectile ownership; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fire Intent model · Theory Fire Intent.
steps.why: Retrieving Fire Intent through the concrete bounded projectile lifecycle case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Fire Intent model · Theory Fire Intent, keep Fire Intent connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Convert action edges and held auto-fire policy into deterministic fire requests separate from object creation.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.
